Convert units

Source: R/ASTR_unit_manipulation.R
convert_concentration_units.Rd

The function is intended to provide "on-the-fly" unit conversions inside functions that require values in a certain unit. Use the respective conversion functions for proper, user-facing unit conversions. It is not intended to be used by users. It converts ALL values in the unit and convertable units of the values intended to be converted to ensure that e.g. normalisation is not performed on subsets.

Usage

convert_concentration_units(df, values, unit_to, ...)

Arguments

df

ASTR object

values

Character vector with column names of the values to be converted.

unit_to

The unit the values should be converted to

...

Additional values passed to the conversion functions. This is primarily intended for conversion into oxides because this function takes a mandatory parameter that does not has a default value.

Details

Only the converted values of the input are intended to be processed further and except for them, columns of the unconverted ASTR object should be made available to the user in the function calling this unit converter.

On this page

Developed by Thomas Rose, Mathias Ayine Abagna, Andrea Acevedo Mejia, Gilberto Artioli, María Florencia Becerra, Adam Kevin Benfer, Lisa Bellemère, Thomas Edward Birch, Karan Desai, Paolo d’Imporzano, Tzilla Eshel, Valerio Gentile, Sabine Klein, Catherine Klesner, Kathryn Murphy, Ben Marwick, Stephen Merkel, Marco Daniele Pelizzari, Alexandra Rodler, Benjamin Sabatini, Clemens Schmid, Berber van der Meulen-van der Veen, Chen Wang, Katrin Julia Westner, Deutsche Forschungsgemeinschaft, Lorentz Center.
